summary: No-ship: the opt-in gateway path can abort the entire Improver run on timeout/network failure, and it silently treats malformed 200 responses as an empty dream reply.

findings.title: Routed dream fetch failures now crash the nightly Improver instead of degrading safely
findings.recommendation: Catch fetch/timeout/network errors for the routed path and either fall back to `defaultRunTurn` or convert the dream pass into a logged/skipped result. As written, `runDreamPhase` awaits `runTurn` without a guard, so an unreachable gateway or `AbortSignal.timeout` rejection aborts `computeDream()` and the whole Improver run.

findings.title: Successful HTTP 200 responses with an unexpected body silently disable dream proposals
findings.recommendation: Validate that the `/chat` response contains a non-empty string reply payload and throw a clear error otherwise. The current `res.json().catch(() => ({}))` plus `data.reply ?? data.text ?? ""` turns malformed or schema-drifted responses into an empty reply, which `runDreamPhase` then parses as zero proposals with no failure signal.
